source_pr.title: expression: pass const bool to all calls for expression.ConstItem
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: close #49755

Problem Summary:

The method `expression.ConstItem` receives a bool argument to indicate whether the expression is a constant consider/not consider different contexts. Many calls use `StatementContext.UseCache` as an input but most of them are necessary. We'd better to use a const bool value as the input argument to make the code easier to understand.

### What changed and how does it work?

- We use `ConstItem(false)` to check that `APPROX_PERCENTILE` should be a constant. It is to make sure its behaviors are the same when in or not in the plan cache.
- We use `ConstItem(true)` in `WrapWithCastAsDecimal` to do some find-grained precision optimization in the expression's build phase. 
- We use `ConstItem(false)` in the vector evaluation implement of function `aes_encrypt`/`aes_decrypt` to do optimize  when key constant.
-  the pattern of `ilike` should be a constant so we use `ConstItem(true)` to check it. 

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [x] Unit test
- [x] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No need to test
  > - [ ] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: prepare stmt1 from 'select a, hex(aes_encrypt(a, ?)) from t1 order by a asc'; (tests/integrationtest/r/expression/plan_cache.result)
failure_signature: prepare stmt1 from 'select a, hex(aes_encrypt(a, ?)) from t1 order by a asc'; (tests/integrationtest/r/expression/plan_cache.result)
root_cause_explanation: The test was flaky because it asserted an exact plan/EXPLAIN output that can vary between runs. Exact plan/EXPLAIN assertions are brittle when optimizer choices can vary. This PR stabilizes it by: We use `ConstItem(false)` to check that `APPROX_PERCENTILE` should be a constant.
fix_pattern: We use `ConstItem(false)` to check that `APPROX_PERCENTILE` should be a constant. It is to make sure its behaviors are the same when in or not in the plan cache. We use `ConstItem(true)` in `WrapWithCastAsDecimal` to do some find-grained precision optimization in the expression's build phase. We use `ConstItem(false)` in the vector evaluation implement of function `aes_encrypt`/`aes_decrypt` to do optimize  when key constant. the pattern of `ilike` should be a constant so we use `ConstItem(true)` to check it.

evidence: evidence: prepare stmt1 from 'select a, hex(aes_encrypt(a, ?)) from t1 order by a asc'; (tests/integrationtest/r/expression/plan_cache.result)
